pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property from 17 to 21 to target the new JDK.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project continues to compile and run as Java 17 code, failing to utilize Java 21 features or enforce Java 21 compatibility.

pom.xml.general_idea: Migrate the annotation API dependency from the legacy javax namespace to the Jakarta namespace.
pom.xml.why_exists: Java 21 (and modern Jakarta EE) uses the jakarta.* namespace; the javax.annotation-api artifact is deprecated/removed in newer ecosystems, leading to potential runtime linkage errors or deprecation warnings in a Jakarta EE context.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add the 'release' flag to the maven-compiler-plugin configuration to enforce multi-release compilation.
pom.xml.why_exists: While source/target still work, the 'release' flag is the modern standard for ensuring bytecode compatibility and API usage matches the target JDK version, preventing accidental use of APIs not available in the target version.

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace the legacy com.sun.xml.bind jaxb-impl dependency with the modern org.glassfish.jaxb jaxb-runtime.
pom.xml.why_exists: The com.sun.xml.bind artifacts are no longer maintained or available in standard repositories for modern JDKs; using the GlassFish group ensures compatibility with Java 21 and modern JAXB implementations.

pom.xml.general_idea: Update the JaCoCo Maven plugin version to a newer snapshot release.

src/main/java/net/mmo/utils/kism/ui/view.general_idea: Annotate serialVersionUID with @Serial to comply with Java 17+ serialization best practices.
src/main/java/net/mmo/utils/kism/ui/view.why_exists: While not strictly a compile error in all setups, modern Java tooling and strict serialization checks (often enabled in Java 17+ projects) require or strongly recommend this annotation to suppress warnings about missing serialVersionUID or to explicitly mark it as part of the serialization contract. Without it, strict builds may fail or warn.

src/main/java/net/mmo/utils/kism/ui/view.general_idea: Replace deprecated List.add(int index, E element) with List.addFirst(E element) for adding to the beginning of a list.
src/main/java/net/mmo/utils/kism/ui/view.why_exists: List.add(int, E) is not deprecated, but addFirst is the preferred modern API for Deque/List operations when adding to the head. This is likely a polishment change driven by static analysis or modernization recipes, not a breaking change as add(int, E) still works.

src/main/java/net/mmo/utils/kism/ui/view.why_exists: String.format is not removed, but String.formatted is the modern, more concise alternative introduced in Java 15. This is a polishment change.
